title: strategy_001_multi_timeframe_trend_alignment
author: manual-transcript-drop
summary_bullets: STRATEGY: Multi-Timeframe Trend Alignment (4h Trend + 1h Entry) ASSET: BTC, ETH TIMEFRAME: 1h entries, 4h trend context MARKET CONDITION: Trending on higher timeframe, pullback on lower timeframe TYPE: Multi-timeframe trend following CONCEPT: The most robust trading strategies align multiple timeframes.
We use the 4h chart to determine trend direction and only take 1h entries in that direction.
This means we need 4h indicator values available on the 1h chart.
The 4h trend tells us WHAT to trade (long or short), the 1h tells us WHEN to enter.
This dramatically reduces false signals because you’re always trading with the bigger picture.

INDICATORS USED (4h context - calculated on 1h using 4x periods): - EMA 200 on 1h (equivalent to ~EMA 50 on 4h) - major trend direction - EMA 800 on 1h (equivalent to ~EMA 200 on 4h) - macro trend filter - ADX calculated on last 56 bars of 1h (equivalent to 14 bars on 4h) - trend strength INDICATORS USED (1h entry): - RSI (period 14) - pullback detection - MACD (fast 12, slow 26, signal 9) - momentum shift timing - EMA 21 - immediate 1h trend - ATR (period 14) - stops and targets 4H TREND DETERM
Price pulls back below EMA 21 on 1h (short-term dip within uptrend) 3.
RSI(14) drops below 40 (pullback has some depth, not just a 1-bar dip) 4.
MACD histogram turns from negative to positive (momentum shifting back up) 5.

extracted_rules: The 4h trend tells us WHAT to trade (long or short), the 1h tells us WHEN to enter.

Price closes back below EMA 21 (bounce is ending, downtrend resuming) EXIT RULES: - Take profit: 2.5x ATR(14) from entry - Stop loss: 1.5x ATR(14) from entry (just beyond the pullback extreme) - Trailing stop: After 1.5 ATR in profit, trail at EMA 21 - Time stop: 24 candles (24 hours) — if trade hasn’t worked by then, the setup failed - Trend invalidation: If EMA 200 crosses below EMA 800 (trend reversal on higher TF), close immediately RISK MANAGEMENT: - Risk per trade: 1% of account - Max conc

strategy_components.description: STRATEGY: Multi-Timeframe Trend Alignment (4h Trend + 1h Entry) ASSET: BTC, ETH TIMEFRAME: 1h entries, 4h trend context MARKET CONDITION: Trending on higher timeframe, pullback on lower timeframe TYPE: Multi-timeframe tr
We use the 4h chart to determine trend direction and only take 1h entries in that direction.
The 4h trend tells us WHAT to trade (long or short), the 1h tells us WHEN to enter.

INDICATORS USED (4h context - calculated on 1h using 4x periods): - EMA 200 on 1h (equivalent to ~EMA 50 on 4h) - major trend direction - EMA 800 on 1h (equivalent to ~EMA 200 on 4h) - macro trend filter - ADX calculated
Price pulls back below EMA 21 on 1h (short-term dip within uptrend) 3.
MACD histogram turns from negative to positive (momentum shifting back up) 5.
Price closes back above EMA 21 (pullback is ending, trend resuming) ENTRY SHORT (1h): 1.
Price rallies above EMA 21 on 1h (short-term bounce within downtrend) 3.
The 4h trend provides the directional edge — you’re trading with the flow of the larger market.
The 1h entry provides timing precision — you’re buying dips and selling rips within that trend.

FALSIFIABLE HYPOTHESIS: BTC/ETH 1h entries aligned with 4h uptrend (EMA 200 > 800 on 1h equivalent) that buy pullbacks below EMA 21 with RSI < 40 and MACD reversal produce profit factor above 1.25 and win rate above 45%.

explicit_conditions: Price closes back below EMA 21 (bounce is ending, downtrend resuming) EXIT RULES: - Take profit: 2.5x ATR(14) from entry - Stop loss: 1.5x ATR(14) from entry (just beyond the pullback extreme) - Trailing stop: After 1.5 ATR in profit, trail at EMA 21 - Time stop: 24 candles (24 hours) — if trade hasn’t worked by then, the setup failed - Trend invalidation: If EMA 200 crosses below EMA 800 (trend reversal on higher TF), close immediately RISK MANAGEMENT: - Risk per trade: 1% of account - Max conc
